name: Ploomber (Data Science Pipeline Framework)

auth.notes: Local framework; Ploomber Cloud for managed execution

performance.notes: Pipeline orchestration; execution time depends on tasks

agent_readiness.notes: Data science pipelines for notebooks and scripts. pipeline.yaml defines DAG of notebooks/scripts. ploomber build runs the pipeline. Incremental builds: only re-runs changed tasks and their dependents. Parallel execution. SQL tasks. ploomber scaffold generates starter project. Notebook as tasks (via papermill). Simpler than Kedro for data science teams. Ploomber Cloud for managed execution. Good for research teams wanting lightweight orchestration without Airflow/Prefect complexity.

gotchas: Smaller community than Kedro or Prefect
Ploomber Cloud (managed service) adds pricing complexity
Less mature than established workflow tools
best_when: Research teams wanting simple notebook/script pipeline DAGs
When Kedro is too opinionated and Prefect too complex
Incremental ML pipeline builds (skip unchanged tasks)
Data science projects where notebooks are first-class citizens
avoid_when: Production ML platforms (Metaflow, Flyte, or Prefect better)
Large engineering teams (Kedro or Dagster better for collaboration)
summary: Ploomber builds data science pipelines from notebooks and scripts via pipeline.yaml DAG config. ploomber build runs the pipeline with incremental builds (only re-runs changed tasks). Parallel execution, SQL tasks, notebook-as-tasks via papermill. Simpler than Kedro for research teams. Good for ML research pipelines where notebooks are primary artifacts. Use Kedro/Prefect for production.
